sourceMovie.title: Intolerable Cruelty
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Intolerable Cruelty for the same reason it broke through in 2003: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Joel Coen keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mr. Right
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Intolerable Cruelty clicked for you, Mr. Right (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Intolerable Cruelty?
faq.answer: Try Mr. Right, Into the Night, Stepmom—each shares DNA with Intolerable Cruelty while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Intolerable Cruelty?
faq.answer: Intolerable Cruelty (2003) is best described as Comedy, Romance, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Intolerable Cruelty worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
